finding: The prefab proposes a per-namespace write queue + version counter in ipc.ts, but this is misaligned with the actual root cause. The 3 critical bugs (IPC-001, IPC-002, IPC-003) are all renderer-side: (1) handleSave in dialog-edit-mcp.tsx:55 is sync and doesn't await onSave, (2) same fire-and-forget in dialog-edit-agent.tsx:59, (3) submitAdd in dialog-select-mcp.tsx:173 calls addMutation.mutate() which calls sdk.client.mcp.add() but never writes to electron-store. Electron-store operations are already synchronous and serialized by Node's event loop — a write queue in the main process does nothing to prevent the renderer-side read-modify-write race. The real fix is: make handleSave async + await onSave in both dialogs, and add electron-store.set("servers", ...) after the SDK add() call. A write queue adds ~50 lines of complexity to the 503-line ipc.ts for zero benefit to the 3 critical bugs.
repair_path: Drop the write queue and version counter from the plan. Replace with: (A) Make dialog-edit-mcp.tsx:55 handleSave async and await props.onSave(entry). (B) Make dialog-edit-agent.tsx:59 handleSave async and await props.onSave(...). (C) In dialog-select-mcp.tsx, add electron-store write inside addMutation's onSuccess callback or at the end of mutationFn to persist newly added MCP servers. These 3 changes fix all critical issues in ~15 lines total, touching only the renderer.

finding: The prefab proposes "Dual SOT Reconciliation — when reading electron-store, also check sync.data as fallback. When writing, update both." This misunderstands the two data sources. sync.data.mcp is SDK runtime connection status (connected/disconnected/failed). electron-store "desktop-mcp-servers" is persistent configuration (commands, URLs, enabled states). They are not dual sources of truth — they serve different purposes. Reading sync.data as a fallback for electron-store would return connection status objects (which have no command/URL fields) instead of config objects, corrupting the UI state. Writing sync.data as part of store writes would mix runtime status with configuration, introducing a new class of bugs. The only missing write is IPC-003.
repair_path: Remove Dual SOT Reconciliation from scope entirely. Keep sync.data.mcp as runtime status only. The 3 critical bugs do not require reconciliation — IPC-003 is fixed by adding a single electron-store write. File a follow-up issue for schema-level validation between store content and sync.data if needed.

finding: The scope boundary is too wide. The plan touches 10 files (ipc.ts, store.ts, 4 dialog components, plus potential preload/types changes) but the 3 critical bugs only require changes in 3 renderer files: dialog-edit-mcp.tsx, dialog-edit-agent.tsx, and dialog-select-mcp.tsx. Adding write queue + version counter + reconciliation to ipc.ts introduces risks to the 60+ existing IPC handlers for zero benefit to the critical bugs. This is a surface-area violation: broadening the change to "architectural IPC reliability" when the bugs are "three specific renderer call-sites forgot await and one forgot to persist."
repair_path: Narrow the boundary to strict minimum: fix 3 renderer call-sites. Defer write queue, version counters, and reconciliation to a separate lane with explicit evidence that renderer-side race conditions are reproducible. The cartography found zero evidence of actual IPC handler-level races — only renderer-level fire-and-forget and missing persist.

finding: Debug visibility for IPC failures is still console.warn-based. dialog-edit-mcp.tsx:55's parent onSave (dialog-select-mcp.tsx:381-392) catches errors and logs "Failed to persist edited server" via console.warn. dialog-edit-agent.tsx:59's parent saveCustomAgents (dialog-manage-agents.tsx:81-97) has try/catch with console.error + toast. Users see the toast for agent failures but NOT for MCP failures. After IPC-001 and IPC-003 fixes, MCP save failures will still be silent (console.warn only, no toast).
repair_path: Add error toast to dialog-select-mcp.tsx's onSave handler for dialog-edit-mcp, matching the agent pattern. Use showToast({ type: "error", title: "Failed to save MCP server", description: e.message }).

finding: Dead preload code removal (IPC-017, installCli bridge) is listed in cartography's priority_high but is already done — commit c8b69c473 removed it. Including it in scope would be redundant. With c8b69c473 confirmed on-dev, this item should be marked complete.
repair_path: Mark IPC-017 as done. Exclude from plan scope.

finding: Testability: The plan has no test scope. None of the 4 source files (dialog-edit-mcp.tsx, dialog-edit-agent.tsx, dialog-select-mcp.tsx, dialog-manage-agents.tsx) have existing test files under packages/app/src/__tests__ or anywhere in the tree. The fix relies on manual QA. Deferring test coverage is acceptable for P0 bugs but must be explicitly stated with a follow-up issue filed for IPC handler integration tests and dialog flow tests.
repair_path: Add a test-debt note: "No IPC handler tests exist for the 4 dialog components. This plan fixes critical bugs without adding tests. File follow-up issue for test coverage: describe IPC handler integration test harness, store persistence tests, concurrent CRUD tests."
